old_claim.note: University Medical Studies provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The scoped Forman-Napier casebooks were produced by astrological practitioners outside the licensed university-physician category, so university medical study is not a prerequisite for this record form.

source_shape.source_locator: The Cambridge critical introduction places Forman and Napier among irregular practitioners and distinguishes them from licensed physicians and university-trained medicine.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The practitioners behind the anchor archive occupied a medical setting that did not require licensed university physician training.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Removing this edge preserves the casebooks as a documentary practice across practitioner types instead of equating record keeping with one professional training route.

ontology_before: Medical Case Records treated university medical study as an enabling prerequisite for early modern serial casebooks.
ontology_after: Early Modern Medical Casebooks are grounded in their written-case lineage and paper medium without requiring university physician training.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: medical_case_records no longer directly depends on university_medical_studies.
would_reject_if: The node is later rescoped specifically to university teaching-hospital records rather than the Forman-Napier practitioner casebooks.
A source establishes university medical training as necessary for producing the anchor casebook archive.
short_reason: The anchor records came from practitioners outside the university-licensed physician category.
